verificationWarning: hFOVDegrees values are calculated from Apple's officially published 35mm-equivalent focal lengths
using the standard rectilinear formula 2×arctan(18/f_equiv), cross-checked against independent
published analyses (see hFOV Research Study 2026-03). Values are NOT direct AVFoundation measurements.
KEY CORRECTION (v1.1.0): All ultrawide (0.5×) entries have been corrected from 120.0° to 108.3°.
Apple's stated '120° field of view' for ultrawide cameras is a DIAGONAL figure, not horizontal.
The horizontal FOV for a 13mm-equivalent ultrawide is 108.3° (calculated) / ~102–108° in video
(accounting for 3–5% stabilization crop). This is confirmed by Crafting Pixels independent analysis
and standard rectilinear optics. Similarly, Apple's '20° FOV' for 5× telephoto is diagonal;
horizontal is 17.1°.
KNOWN APPLE BUG (iOS 16.0, iPhone 14 Pro only, FIXED in iOS 16.1): videoFieldOfView for the 1×
wide camera returned ~39–41° instead of ~67–74° due to the 48MP quad-pixel sensor confusing the
internal FOV calculation. Confirmed fixed via Apple Feedback FB11545441. Apps targeting iOS 16.0
on iPhone 14 Pro should hardcode corrected values or require iOS 16.1+.
iPhone 17 Pro/Max telephoto note: The 4× label is the physical 100mm lens. The 8× label is a
2× sensor crop of that lens. AVFoundation's videoFieldOfView reports the base format FOV (~19.9°)
regardless of zoom factor — effective 8× FOV (~10°) must be computed as 2×arctan(tan(baseFOV/2)/2).
This is documented API behavior, not a bug.
To upgrade an entry to hFOVVerified: true, it must meet AT LEAST ONE of:
(a) Direct measurement on physical hardware via AVCaptureDevice.activeFormat.videoFieldOfView,
(b) Confirmation against a minimum of 3 independent, citable published sources.
modelIdentifiers for iPhone 17 series entries are unverified and should be confirmed against a physical device or the Apple model identifier registry.
notes: The 2× label on 48MP-capable devices (iPhone 14 Pro and later, iPhone 15 and later) is a sensor crop from the main wide camera, not a separate physical camera. It is typed 'telephoto' for UI consistency with physical telephoto entries.

On iPhone 17 Pro/Max, the 4× telephoto is the physical 100mm lens (48MP sensor). The 8× mode is a 2× sensor crop of that lens yielding effective 200mm. videoFieldOfView returns the base 4× FOV (~19.9°) for both; the 8× effective FOV (~10°) must be derived by computation.
This file lives in the repo root as a working draft. Per ADDENDUM-G Section G.2, the production copy belongs in the app bundle alongside lenses.json and ptz_cameras.json.

cameras.hFOVSource: calculated — 2×arctan(18/13)=108.3°; Apple's stated 120° is diagonal not horizontal (hFOV study 2026-03)
